provenance.source_corpus: Long‐term effects of prairie restoration on plant community structure and native population dynamics

source_spans.text: We found that plots that received supplemental seeding continued to exhibit higher richness of native species than those left unseeded
We found that plots that received supplemental seeding continued to exhibit higher richness of native species than those left unseeded
addition of native seed effectively increased both the richness and abundance of native species
plots that received applications of grass-specific herbicide had lower abundances of exotic grasses than the remaining disturbance treatments
plots that received applications of grass-specific herbicide had lower abundances of exotic grasses than the remaining disturbance treatments
three perennial forb species (Eriophyllum lanatum, Achillea millefolium, and Ranunculus occidentalis) responded more positively to both of the burn treatments
three perennial forb species (Eriophyllum lanatum, Achillea millefolium, and Ranunculus occidentalis) responded more positively to both of the burn treatments
three perennial forb species (Eriophyllum lanatum, Achillea millefolium, and Ranunculus occidentalis) responded more positively to both of the burn treatments
three perennial forb species (Eriophyllum lanatum, Achillea millefolium, and Ranunculus occidentalis) responded more positively to both of the burn treatments
native grass species, Festuca roemeri, had greater cover in treatments that included the grass-specific herbicide sethoxydim
there is evidence that all eight species colonized new plots since the last round of monitoring... at least 25% of the currently occupied plots are a result of population spread
